title: Setting Up the Multi-Layer Network
concept: Coding the linear layers and choosing activation functions (like ReLU) for hidden layers.

exercises.prompt: What is the goal of our image classifier project?
exercises.explain: The goal of our image classifier project is to classify handwritten digits, ensuring we understand the target classes and overall objectives.

cards.title: What is a Multi-Layer Network?
cards.body: A multi-layer network consists of multiple layers of nodes (neurons) that learn to process inputs, such as images, step by step. Each layer adds more complexity to help the model understand data.

cards.title: Linear Layer
cards.body: A linear layer applies a mathematical transformation to its input, using weights and biases to change data into new forms. It's like changing ingredients in a recipe to create a different dish.

cards.title: Building a Tower with Blocks
cards.body: Think of each linear layer as a level in a tower. Each level shapes the tower differently, adding height and complexity, just like layers shape the final outcome of our model.

cards.title: Using ReLU Activation
cards.body: ReLU (Rectified Linear Unit) transforms negative values to zero, keeping positive values unchanged. Imagine it like a filter that only allows bright colors to pass through while blocking dark ones.

cards.title: Myth: All Activation Functions are the Same
cards.body: Not all activation functions work the same way. Some are best for certain tasks, like ReLU for hidden layers, which helps with faster learning and better performance.

cards.title: Comparison of Layers and Activation Functions
cards.body: Let's look at how different layers and activation functions work together in a network.

left.text: Transforms data using weights and biases.

right.text: Keeps positive values, turns negatives to zero.

exercises.prompt: What does the ReLU activation function do?
exercises.explain: ReLU (Rectified Linear Unit) function turns negative values to zero, helping the model learn effectively.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A linear layer is responsible for transforming data in a network.
exercises.explain: Linear layers transform the data by applying mathematical operations, crucial for building networks.

exercises.prompt: A linear layer uses ______ and biases to transform data.
exercises.sentence: A linear layer uses weights and biases to transform data.
exercises.explain: Weights are crucial as they determine how the input data is adjusted during processing.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following describes the function of ReLU?

options.text: Filters negative numbers out, only keeping positives
Standardizes all values to be between 0 and 1

exercises.explain: ReLU specifically functions by transforming negative values to zero and retaining positive ones, enhancing the learning process.

exercises.prompt: What are the two critical elements for setting up a multi-layer network?

options.text: Specifying target classes and using ReLU for activations
Collecting only positive data and defining biases only
Focusing on physical structure and avoiding layers
Choosing any random activation and skipping linear layers

exercises.explain: Key elements include specifying the target classes (like digits) and selecting appropriate activation functions like ReLU, which help ensure the network learns effectively.
